title: Create: Oxidized plus
description: Datapack allowing oxidizing Copper things Using Spouts
body:
![Copper block oxidation plant, with conveyor belts, pipes, vaults, filters, funnels](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/af3a57f0dcfb671aa2a8a690ecead5075e2086f2.jpeg) # About [Create: Oxidized](https://modrinth.com/mod/create_oxidized) is an add-on for the Create mod. It introduces new recipes for Oxidizing Copper, allowing players to Oxidize Copper Faster. This **datapack** adds more recipes to the list.
![a list of copper items](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/6deb24d540267d9a8a6ea23e14ff1d3d10d864ff.jpeg)![copper golem walking](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/5eb41d30e9dbd9cca9b15f80ea62dd509dd3031f.gif)copper golem not included (It has its own mechanics)
only unwaxed blocks...
## Recipe Fill spouts with 500mb of water onto copper block variant. (I left it as is while I decide). ![recipe displayed by JEI](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/7d4157fa123c10c319aa5ed868163e81ee3f908c.jpeg) Tip: Use [Just Enough Items(JEI)](https://modrinth.com/mod/jei) To View The Recipe In-Game
## ⚙️ Supported Version 1.21.1 NeoForge
1.21.1 Create
0.1.2 (above) Create:Oxidized ## 🧩 Installation Guide ### Just place it in your datapack folder. But for those who don't know how to do it: 1. Go to where you have Minecraft installed. 2. Find the saves folder. 3. You'll see folders with the names of your worlds; choose the one you want to use the datapack on. 4. Place the file in the datapacks folder.
Something like this: C:\User\ .minecraft\saves\new_world\datapacks Just leave it there, done! [](https://www.curseforge.com/members/kentapainter/projects)[]()[]()
